'''Craig Kopczak''' (born 20 December 1986) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 2008's [[RFL Championship|National League One]]. Kopczak has also played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Craig Kopczak was born in [[Bradford]], West Yorkshire, England.

Craig is married to Victoria and they have two children, Poppy and Harvey.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Daily Star|title=Check out Super League's finest WAGs|url=https://www.dailystar.co.uk/news/latest-news/super-league-wags-rugby-league-16989863.amp|date=25 Feb 2017|accessdate=26 Dec 2020}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Bradford===
Kopczak made his Bradford  début in 2006. He spent at period in 2008 on loan at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]],<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/mobile/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/7243287.stm|website=BBC Sport|date=28 September 2015|access-date=13 February 2008|title=Bulls prop Copczak to join Halifax}}{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> and later established himself as an important first-team player for Bradford. He scored his first try for the club against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in 2008, and scored his first career hat-trick against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-wakefield/29920|website=Sky Sports|date=6 March 2011|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Bulls too strong for Cats}}</ref>

===Huddersfield===
Kopczak signed a three-year deal<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/45939966|website=BBC Sport|date=22 October 2018|access-date=25 October 2012|title=Huddersfield Giants: Craig Kopczak signs three-year deal}}</ref> with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] after he left the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] club.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12213/8063361/kopczak-walks-out-on-bulls|website=Sky Sports|date=8 September 2012|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Kopczak walks out on Bulls}}</ref> He played for the club for three years and was a member of the team that won the [[League Leaders' Shield]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12213/8063361/kopczak-walks-out-on-bulls|website=The Guardian|first=Andy|last=Wilson|date=1 September 2013|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Huddersfield Giants finish on top in England for first time since 1932}}</ref>

===Salford===
Kopczak joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] ahead of the [[2016 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-prop-craig-kopczak-10146670|website=Yorkshire Live|date=28 September 2015|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Huddersfield Giants prop Craig Kopczak set for move to Salford Red Devils|first=Peter|last=Barrow-Hudd}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/21930/10027003/craig-kopczak-to-captain-wales-at-european-championships|website=Sky Sports|date=13 October 2015|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Craig Kopczak to captain Wales in European Championship}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
Kopczak joined Wakefield Trinity in October 2018 on a one-year deal<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/45939966|website=BBC Sport|date=22 October 2018|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Wakefield sign prop Craig Kopczak from Salford on one-year deal}}</ref> for the [[2019 Super League season]], after Salford accepted a transfer fee from the West Yorkshire club. He signed a contract extension in March 2019,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/47609903|website=BBC Sport|date=18 March 2019|access-date=7 November 2024|title=Craig Kopczak: Wakefield Trinity prop extends deal until 2020}}</ref> remaining at the club until the end of the [[2020 Super League season]].

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 5 December 2020, it was announced that he would join [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the [[2021 RFL Championship|2021 season]].<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=5 Dec 2020|accessdate=26 Dec 2020|title=FEATHERSTONE ROVERS SIGN CRAIG KOPCZAK|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57467/featherstone-rovers-sign-craig-kopczak}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, he played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=29 May 2022}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 23 October 2023, he joined [[Oldham RLFC]] for the 2024 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Oct 2023|title=Former Wales captain Craig Kopczak joins League One club for 2024: 'A very strong squad is being built for next year'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-wales-captain-craig-kopczak-joins-league-one-club-oldham-for-2024}}</ref> Oldham were crowned [[RFL League One]] champions in [[2024 RFL League One|2024]] and automatically promoted to the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theoldhamtimes.co.uk/sport/24566750.turner-proud-yeds-achieve-unthinkable/|title=Turner so proud as Yeds achieve the 'unthinkable'|website=The Oldham Times|date=6 September 2024|access-date=7 November 2024}}</ref>

On 31 March 2025, Kopczak announced that he was retiring from Rugby League with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-stalwart-announces-retirement-with-honest-admission-made|title=Stalwart announces retirement with honest admission|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=31 March 2025}}</ref>

==Wales==
Although born in England, Craig represents Wales at international level due to his heritage. He made his Wales début in 2007, and scored his first Wales try in a match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2009.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.cymrurl.com/home.php |title=Cymru RL |accessdate=2009-10-20 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20091214094214/http://www.cymrurl.com/home.php |archivedate=2009-12-14 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=2382 |title=Engage Super League |accessdate=2009-02-25 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110108085305/http://superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=2382 |archivedate=2011-01-08 }}</ref>

He featured for Wales in the [[2011 Four Nations]].
He was Wales player of the tournament during the Four Nations, whilst also winning Bradford's Player of the Year 2011.

He [[Captain (sports)|captain]]ed [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/15064/meet-the-team-craig-kopczak|website=Wales Rugby League|title=Meet the Team - Craig Kopczak|accessdate=26 Dec 2020}}</ref> in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] and [[2015 European Cup]]. He captained the team again in the following year's [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe|2017 World Cup qualifiers]].

Craig retired from International Rugby League in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44082894|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Wales captain Craig Kopczak retires from internationals to focus on Salford|date=11 May 2018}}</ref>
